content.problemStatement:
You are given arrays AAA and BBB, each consisting of NNN **strings**, and a positive integer KKK.

For all 1≤i≤N1\le i \le N1≤i≤N, both AiA_iAi​ and BiB_iBi​ consist of characters from 000 to 999 (both inclusive).

You can perform the following types of operations on array AAA:


Type 111: Choose two indices iii and jjj (i≠j)(i\neq j)(i=j) and swap any character of AiA_iAi​ with any character of AjA_jAj​. The cost of this operation is 000.
Type 222: Choose an index iii and replace **one** character of AiA_iAi​ with any character from 000 to 999 (both inclusive). The cost of this operation is 111.
For example, let A=[24,30,51]A = [24, 30, 51]A=[24,30,51]. A valid sequence of operations can be:


Swap 444 in A1=24A_1 = 24A1​=24 with 000 in A2=30A_2 = 30A2​=30 to obtain [20,34,51][20, 34, 51][20,34,51].
Swap 000 in A1=20A_1 = 20A1​=20 with 555 in A3=51A_3 = 51A3​=51 to obtain [25,34,01][25, 34, 01][25,34,01].
Replace 000 in A3=01A_3 = 01A3​=01 with 111 to obtain [25,34,11][25, 34, 11][25,34,11].

The cost of the above sequence of operations is 111.
Find whether we can convert the array AAA to array BBB using **any** (possibly zero) number of operations with cost ≤K\le K≤K.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK, the size of array and the maximum cost of operations.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated strings A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
The third line of each test case contains NNN space-separated strings B1,B2,…,BNB_1,B_2,\ldots,B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​.




Output Format
For each test case, print `YES` if it is possible to convert array AAA to BBB using **any** (possibly zero) number of operations with cost ≤K\le K≤K. Else, print `NO`.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `yEs`, `yes`, `Yes`, and `YES` will all be treated as identical).
constraints: 1 ≤ N ≤ 10^5 (sum of N over all test cases ≤ 10^5)
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases.
For each test case:
- The first line contains two integers N and K.
- The second line contains N strings A1, A2, ..., AN separated by spaces.
- The third line contains N strings B1, B2, ..., BN separated by spaces.
All strings consist only of digits '0'‑'9'.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if it is possible to transform array A into array B with total cost not exceeding K, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: lengths match for each position. The multiset of digits is already identical, so zero replacements are needed (cost 0 ≤ 2).\nTest case 2: A1 has length 1 while B1 has length 2, therefore transformation is impossible regardless of K.\nTest case 3: All lengths match. Counting digits gives a deficit of 1 (one extra '8' needed), which can be fixed with one replacement (cost 1 ≤ K=1). Hence YES.

solution.approach: For each test case we first check that every corresponding pair of strings has the same length; a swap never changes a string's length, so a mismatch makes the transformation impossible. If all lengths match, we count how many of each digit appear in the whole array A and in the whole array B. Swaps are free, therefore we can freely match any existing digit of A with the same digit needed in B. The only costly operation is to replace a digit that is missing. The minimal number of replacements required equals the total deficit of digits: sum over d∈[0..9] of max(0, cntB[d] - cntA[d]). If this deficit ≤ K we answer YES, otherwise NO.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total number of characters) per test case, i.e., O( Σ|Ai| ).
